metadata.notes: agent=yes daemon=full driver=gap:wait_turn â€” smallest round-trip; parser emits user_messageâ†’working and a terminal empty PLANNER_RESPONSEâ†’ready, but the interactive driver's wait_turn is a stub.

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full
driver_capability: gap:wait_turn

## Reasoning

basic-turn is the smallest round-trip: one user prompt, one assistant reply, no tool use, asserting the `ready â†’ working â†’ ready` arc with `TotalTokens > 0` after the turn.

### agent_supports: yes

The `agy` CLI (v1.0.10, native binary at ~/.local/bin/agy) runs a plain conversational round-trip out of the box. The four real sample conversations under `~/.gemini/antigravity-cli/brain/` each open with a `USER_EXPLICIT/USER_INPUT` line (the `<USER_REQUEST>` prompt) and close with a terminal `MODEL/PLANNER_RESPONSE` carrying no `tool_calls` â€” exactly the basic-turn shape. The model (`Gemini 3.5 Flash`) is harvested from the `<USER_SETTINGS_CHANGE>` block on the first turn. No special mode is needed; `agy --print` (headless) and the interactive REPL both run a single promptâ†’reply turn.

### daemon_capability: full

Source = `FilesUnderRoot{Dir: .gemini/antigravity-cli/brain, ExtraDirs: [.gemini/antigravity/brain], SessionIDFromPath, JSONLineParser{Parser}}` (`core/adapters/inbound/agents/antigravity/agent.go`). Walking the user-observable lifecycle signal against `parser.go`:

1. **`ready â†’ working â†’ ready`** â€” observed correctly. `parseUserInput` emits a `user_message` event for the opening `USER_INPUT` line (and sets `ClearToolNames`), driving `ready â†’ working`. The turn's terminal line is a `MODEL/PLANNER_RESPONSE` with NO `tool_calls`, which `parsePlannerResponse` reclassifies as `EventType = "turn_done"` (parser.go ~L113-118), the authoritative settle to `ready`. For a tool-free turn the topology is exactly `ready â†’ working â†’ ready`, final state `ready`, zero tool calls â€” matching `Tool calls: â‰¤ 0`. The session_id is the `<conv-id>` directory (SessionIDFromPath); the transcript is written from the first line, so the session is born directly in `ready` (single-birth, like claudecode/kiro-cli â€” NO transient `proc-<PID>` presession row, unlike codex).

2. **`TotalTokens > 0`** â€” NOT observable for antigravity, and this is NOT a daemon bug. Tokens/cost are not in the JSONL transcript at all (they live in a per-conversation SQLite protobuf store); the parser docstring states plainly "Tokens/cost are not in the JSONL â€¦ so the parser surfaces no usage" (parser.go ~L30-33). Per the caveats-over-downgrades rule, the canonical STATE arc is fully met â†’ daemon stays `full`, and the token gap is a caveat. This mirrors the sibling kiro-cli basic-turn cell exactly (sidecar-only metrics; `tokens_nonzero:false, cost_nonzero:false`); the claudecode/codex siblings also note `TotalTokens > 0` is not expressible in the phase DSL.

Both halves resolve cleanly: the lifecycle arc is emitted today â†’ `daemon=full`.

### driver_capability: gap:wait_turn

The interactive driver (`replaydata/agents/antigravity/driver-interactive.sh`) is a scaffold: its header documents that `DRIVE_ELICITS="send slash sleep"` is the authoritative semantic list of genuinely-ported primitives, and the `wait_turn()` function is a stub (`not_implemented wait_turn` â€” driver-interactive.sh ~L168-170). The basic-turn recipe needs `send` + `wait_turn` (block until the terminal empty PLANNER_RESPONSE) + a trailing `sleep`. `wait_turn` is not ported, so the recipe needs a step type the driver lacks â†’ `driver=gap:wait_turn`. This is TOOLING work, not an observability limit: `record` ports the `wait_turn` seam (the turn-done poll over the transcript's terminal empty PLANNER_RESPONSE) plus the launch_repl session-id/transcript-path resolution SEAM (also a documented TODO), then drives. An INTERACTIVE recipe is required: a headless `agy --print` run exits at turn completion, before the daemon can observe the terminal `working â†’ ready` edge â€” the same shape that keeps the claudecode/codex/gemini-cli/kiro-cli basic-turn cells interactive.

## What irrlicht does NOT see (narrow)

`TotalTokens > 0` has no observable source in the antigravity transport (tokens live only in the per-conversation SQLite protobuf store the parser does not read) â€” asserted false in the observations vector, not a daemon bug.
caveats: Tokens/cost are NOT in the antigravity JSONL (they live in a per-conversation SQLite protobuf store the parser does not read), so the scenario's TotalTokens > 0 criterion is not observable; observations assert tokens_nonzero:false, cost_nonzero:false. Same as the sibling kiro-cli cell. NOT a daemon bug â€” the lifecycle arc is fully observed.

Model IS observable for antigravity (harvested from the <USER_SETTINGS_CHANGE> block â€” 'Gemini 3.5 Flash' in the live samples), unlike kiro-cli's empty model. But the exact NormalizeModelName output is provisional; left out of observations for record to settle from the recording.

Provisional on emission: daemon=full for the lifecycle arc is grounded in the parser read + four real sample transcripts under ~/.gemini/antigravity-cli/brain/, not yet a live recording. The terminal empty-PLANNER_RESPONSE turn_done and the single-birth shape are confirmed in the samples; record promotes provisionalâ†’settled.

driver=gap:wait_turn: the interactive driver is a scaffold (DRIVE_ELICITS='send slash sleep'); wait_turn and the launch_repl session-id/transcript-path resolution are stubs. record ports both before driving.

sources.note: parseUserInput â†’ user_message (readyâ†’working, ClearToolNames); parsePlannerResponse with NO tool_calls â†’ turn_done (~L113-118, workingâ†’ready); docstring ~L30-33: tokens/cost are NOT in the JSONL (per-conversation SQLite protobuf store), parser surfaces no usage.

Source = FilesUnderRoot{Dir:.gemini/antigravity-cli/brain, ExtraDirs:[.gemini/antigravity/brain], SessionIDFromPath, JSONLineParser}; ExactName{agy}; PIDForSession=DiscoverPID (optional CLI liveness enrichment).

Real sample: USER_EXPLICIT/USER_INPUT opens, terminal MODEL/PLANNER_RESPONSE with content:null and no tool_calls closes â†’ the basic-turn shape; <USER_SETTINGS_CHANGE> records model 'Gemini 3.5 Flash'.

DRIVE_ELICITS="send slash sleep" (authoritative ported-primitive list per the header); wait_turn() is a stub (not_implemented wait_turn, ~L168-170); launch_repl has a TODO SEAM to resolve session_id+transcript path â†’ driver=gap:wait_turn.

script.text: Reply with exactly the word: ok
